landing.heroSub: Lock the decision, reasoning and confidence you have today. When the outcome arrives, compare Then / Now and see what should change next time.
landing.step1Desc: Write what you are deciding, why, what you predict and how confident you are. The original thinking is locked.
landing.step2Desc: Set a replay date and add it to your calendar, without an account or server-side notifications.
landing.step3Desc: Separate whether the prediction matched from whether the outcome was simply good or bad.
landing.forWho: For decisions that can be checked.
landing.profileProfDesc: Work decisions: suppliers, projects, teams, strategic moves.
landing.profileStudentDesc: Decisions about studying, school, assignments, activities, goals.
landing.profilePersonalDesc: Big life decisions: job change, purchases, turning points.
landing.eyebrow: A decision journal for professional decisions
landing.heroCardAria: Example of a locked professional decision
landing.demoDecisionTitle: Should we change our Search Ads landing pages?
landing.demoChoice: Run a four-week test with category-specific product landing pages.
landing.demoPrediction: Product-page engagement will increase by at least 18%.
landing.demoTitle: The value is in the comparison.
landing.demoSub: You are not merely keeping notes. You are checking what you believed before you knew the result.
landing.demoReason: 91% of clicks land on the homepage without matching the intent of the search query.
landing.demoOutcome: Engagement rose by 23%, but checkout conversion stayed almost unchanged.
landing.demoLesson: The landing page improved intent, but checkout friction remained the main constraint.
landing.howTitle: A simple loop, not another complex dashboard.
landing.howSub: Five essential questions now. One honest comparison later.
landing.forWhoSub: Useful when there is a prediction, hypothesis or criterion that reality will eventually test. Not for every small daily choice.
landing.useCase1Desc: Capture the hypothesis and expected result before seeing the metrics.
landing.useCase2Desc: Review the choice against the criteria you set, not against hindsight impressions.
landing.useCase3Desc: See where confidence was too high and which information was missing.
landing.privacySub: There is no account or decision-data server. Decisions stay in your browser and can be exported as a backup.

onboarding.step1Title: Which examples should the app use?
onboarding.step1Sub: The default is professional use. You can change the examples without changing the core Then / Now mechanism.
onboarding.changeLater: You can change this anytime in Settings.
onboarding.step2Title3: You come back when the outcome shows up.
onboarding.professionalDesc: Predictions, projects, suppliers, campaigns and team decisions.
onboarding.studentDesc: Study plans, assignments, goals and education choices.
onboarding.personalDesc: Purchases, job changes and other personal decisions with a checkable outcome.

dashboard.calibrationSub: Comparing original confidence with whether predictions matched.
dashboard.calibrationNoData: Early data is directional. At least 5 replays are needed for a more stable pattern.
dashboard.emptyDesc: It takes about two minutes to lock the prediction and decide when you will review it.

wizard.step1Title: What kind of decision is this?
wizard.step1Sub: Pick a template or start blank.
wizard.step2Sub: What you write here will be locked. You can't change it later.
wizard.step3Title: What do you expect to happen?
wizard.step3Sub: These are the points we'll check at replay time.
wizard.titleField: 1. Which decision or hypothesis are you testing?
wizard.titlePlaceholder: e.g. Change landing pages for Search Ads
wizard.reason: 3. Why do you think this is the right choice?
wizard.expected: 4. What exactly do you expect to happen?
wizard.confidence: 5. How confident are you in the prediction?
wizard.secondOpinion: Which second opinion or opposing view did you consider?
wizard.advanced: Optional detail for a stricter review
wizard.lockConfirmBody: After locking, you cannot change the decision, prediction or confidence. This protects the comparison from hindsight edits.
wizard.chooseAtLeast: You need a title to continue.
wizard.optionPlaceholder: e.g. Keep every ad landing on the homepage
wizard.coreTitle: Capture the decision as you see it today.
wizard.coreSub: Write only what you will need for a fair comparison later. Optional detail comes in the next step.
wizard.titleHelper: A short title you will recognize immediately.
wizard.choiceCore: 2. What are you deciding to do?
wizard.choiceHelper: State the specific action or option you are committing to test.
wizard.choicePlaceholder: e.g. Run a four-week test with category-specific landing pages.
wizard.reasonHelper: Record the main evidence or reasoning that leads you here.
wizard.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. Most clicks land on the homepage and lose relevance to the search query.
wizard.expectedHelper: Make the prediction specific enough to check.
wizard.expectedPlaceholder: e.g. Product-page engagement will increase by at least 18%.
wizard.confidenceQuestion: Not how much you want it to work. How likely do you think it is to happen?
wizard.replayEyebrow: The return is part of the decision
wizard.replayTitle: When will a real outcome exist?
wizard.replaySub: Choose a date when you will have enough evidence to compare Then / Now. After locking, you can add the replay directly to your calendar.
wizard.replayWhenHelper: Do not choose too early. You need evidence, not a first impression.
wizard.successHelper: Which threshold or result will count as success?
wizard.successPlaceholder: e.g. +15% engagement with no drop in checkout conversion
wizard.alternativesHelper: Useful when you want to remember what else you considered.

detail.deleteConfirm: This will be deleted permanently. It can't be undone.
detail.challengeDesc: Send a link to a colleague before the outcome is known so they can record their own prediction without seeing yours.
detail.reminderDesc: The replay is scheduled for {{date}}. The reminder is created directly in your calendar.
detail.createdSub: The replay is set for {{date}}. Add it to your calendar before leaving this page.
detail.replayReadySub: Record the actual outcome first. The original prediction will appear afterward.
detail.challengePrivacy: The link contains the title, context, options and prediction you entered. Do not use it with confidential information.

replay.title: Replay — {{title}}
replay.outcomeRating: How good was the outcome for your objective?
replay.unexpected: Which unexpected factor affected the outcome?
replay.step2Title: What did the difference teach you?
replay.wouldRepeat: With what you knew then, would you make the same decision again?
replay.whatChange: What would you change in the next similar decision?
replay.noHindsight: First record what happened without rereading the original prediction. It will appear on the next screen, reducing hindsight adjustment.
replay.step1Sub: Separate two questions: did the prediction match, and was the final outcome good?
replay.outcomePlaceholder: Record the main facts or metrics before interpreting them.
replay.expectationMatchHelper: This measures prediction versus reality, not whether you liked the outcome.
replay.outcomeRatingHelper: A correct prediction can concern a bad outcome, and vice versa.
replay.step2Sub: Now record what worked, what surprised you and what you would change.

comparison.title: Then / Now
comparison.observationCalibratedGood: The prediction matched and the original {{c}}% confidence was reasonable.
comparison.observationOveroptimistic: You state higher confidence than gets confirmed. Being slightly more conservative may help.
comparison.lessonTitle: The lesson I will carry into the next decision
comparison.lessonPlaceholder: e.g. The landing page improved intent, but checkout remained the main constraint.
comparison.matchNotRecorded: Not recorded in this older version
comparison.observationOverconfident: The prediction did not match despite {{c}}% confidence. Review which assumption was treated as more certain than it was.
comparison.observationUnderconfident: The prediction matched despite only {{c}}% confidence. You may be underrating evidence that you evaluate well.
comparison.observationPartial: The prediction matched only partly. The useful work is to separate which part held and which part did not.

settings.title: Settings
settings.restoreQuestion: {{count}} decisions will be imported. Replace existing?
settings.corruptFile: This file isn't a valid Decision Replay backup.
settings.deleteAllConfirm: All your decisions will be deleted. Download a backup first?

about.title: What Decision Replay is
about.p1: A privacy-first decision journal for professional decisions, predictions and hypotheses that can be checked later.
about.p2: It does not tell you what to decide. It locks your reasoning before the outcome is known and helps compare the original prediction with reality.
about.isNot1: It isn't a decision coach or advisor.
about.isNot2: It isn't a tool for medical, legal or financial decisions.
about.isNot3: It doesn't use AI to guess outcomes.
about.isNot4: It doesn't collect anything from you.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.summary: Decision Replay does not send the content of your decisions to a server. Decisions are stored only in your device browser.
privacy.detail1: There is no backend for decisions, user accounts or sync.
privacy.detail2: No analytics cookies or trackers are used.
privacy.detail4: Clearing browser data will also delete decisions. Use the local backup in Settings.
privacy.detail5: The code is open source (MIT license).
privacy.sensitive: Do not enter client names, personal data, health information or confidential company details. Use general descriptions and anonymous titles.

sensitiveWarning: Better not to write names, health info or sensitive details. Use general descriptions if unsure.

professional-general.name: General work decision
professional-general.desc: Flexible template for any decision at work.

professional-time.name: Project time estimate
professional-time.desc: How long will it take? What's the critical path?

student-study.name: Study method
student-study.desc: Which study method to try for an exam.

personal-job.name: Job change
personal-job.desc: One of the biggest decisions you can make.

personal-purchase.name: Big purchase
personal-purchase.desc: For purchases you'd like to evaluate later.

challenge.invalidDesc: This challenge link is invalid or has expired.
challenge.title: Challenge
challenge.yourPrediction: What do you think will happen?
challenge.ctaDesc: Want to track your own decisions?

insights.title: Confidence vs reality
insights.calibrated: Well calibrated! Your confidence matches your outcomes.
insights.overconfident: Confidence is {{gap}} points higher than prediction match.
insights.underconfident: Confidence is {{gap}} points lower than prediction match.
insights.subtitle: Prediction match scores 100% when it matched, 50% when it partly matched and 0% when it did not.
insights.aligned: Original confidence is close to the match rate of your predictions so far.
insights.earlyData: Early signal — at least 5 replays are needed for a more stable conclusion.

share.title: Anonymous Decision Replay card
share.tweet: I was {{confidence}}% sure about "{{title}}". See what actually happened.
share.privacyNote: The card is anonymous by default and excludes the decision, prediction and actual outcome.
share.includeTitleHint: Enable only when the title contains no confidential information.
share.postText: I replayed a decision: original confidence {{confidence}}%, prediction result “{{match}}”.

shell.localData: Decisions are stored only in this browser.
